hallucinations.description: The paper describes a complex multi-objective framework including reinforcement learning (policy gradients, reward R = Accuracy -  eta·CacheSize), Gumbel-Softmax sampling, MAML for meta-learning, and coverage loss with specific equations. The actual code implements only a simple Adam-optimized loss combining efficiency (retention_rate**2) and coverage terms, with no RL, no policy gradient, no MAML, and no Gumbel-Softmax.
hallucinations.evidence: Section 3.3 (Joint Optimization Framework) vs. run_experiment.py:train_policy_network (lines 280-340) which only computes efficiency_loss + coverage_loss and calls loss.backward() + optimizer.step().

hallucinations.description: Multiple citations reference non-existent or future papers (e.g., SlimInfer Long et al. 2025 arXiv:2508.06447, PLPHP Meng et al. 2025, ThinkPrune Hou et al. 2025). These do not correspond to real published works.
hallucinations.evidence: References 7,9,10 and inline mentions of SlimInfer (Long et al., 2025), PLPHP (Meng et al., 2025).

overall_assessment: Hallucinations were found: the methodology section fabricates training components (RL + MAML) absent from the actual implementation, and several citations point to nonexistent 2025 papers. Experimental results themselves are consistent with the executed code.
